name: Bark Barbecue
source: The Infatuation / Bushwick Daily / Time Out July 2026
note: Texas-Dominican BBQ flagship from pitmaster Ruben Santana, who pairs Central Texas smoking technique with his Dominican heritage. Opened Wednesday July 1, 2026 in an 8,000 sq ft space seating nearly 300 — four custom smokers behind a glass wall, illuminated orange-tiled bar, communal tables and a stage. Brisket, pork ribs, smoked carnitas, pulled pork, chicharron and rib tips sold by weight, plus the Tres Golpes sandwich. Beer/wine held up by a licensing delay at open.

name: Sailor & Siren
note: Maine lobster-roll spot that grew out of Natalie Borowski's 2025 roaming pop-up. Opened July 3, 2026 in a butter-yellow former hardware store at the corner of Calyer St. Every roll packs the meat of a full 1.25 lb lobster sourced from the family wharf in Maine, with optional caviar and uni toppings; also hot dogs and whoopie pies.

name: Sushi Roll Club
note: Sit-down sister to the Silver Rice and Don Udon operations, opened early July 2026. Addresses the gap in Silver Rice's takeout model with indoor seating and real plates — rolls, chirashi bowls and sashimi platters. Reviewed by The Infatuation July 1, 2026.

name: Prosciutto
source: The Infatuation / EV Grieve / What Now NY 2026
note: Cozy 20-seat (45 max), walk-in-only Tuscan trattoria from co-owners Gabriele Tosi and Mattia Casarin, formerly exec chef and head chef at Fiaschetteria Pistoia. In the former Evil Katsu space. Lasagna alla bolognese, pork belly with beans and salsa verde, Sardinian gnocchi in prosciutto ragu; plans for acoustic jazz and karaoke. Opened summer 2026.

name: The Museum Bar + Gift Shop
note: Quirky new Bushwick cocktail bar with a museum-and-gift-shop conceit — the menu includes an ant-and-cricket Negroni. Listed among The Infatuation's just-added July 2026 openings.

name: Only Child
note: Late-night cocktail bar from the Frog Wine Bar team, one block from Frog in Bed-Stuy — martinis and snacks like popcorn and clam dip, open until 2am weekends. A May 2026 DOB work-stoppage order over electrical/plumbing permits delayed the planned June open; treat opening date as unconfirmed.

name: Hidden Rivers
source: Park Slope Pulse / Andrea Strong Substack / Instagram June 2026
note: Craft-beer, wine, and cocktail bar with 12 rotating taps from Smith & Vine owners Patrick Watson and Michele Pravda with partner Dhruv Mohindra. Opened Tuesday June 9, 2026 at 291 Nevins St (between Sackett & Union) in Gowanus. Named for the buried streams that once ran through the neighborhood.

name: Sushi Counter
source: Park Slope Pulse / NYC for Free / Instagram June 2026
note: Australian-style budget sushi-handroll chain's Brooklyn debut on the Prospect Heights / Park Slope border at the corner of Flatbush & Dean. Opened Saturday June 6, 2026 — first 50 customers got a free lunch, complimentary miso soup all day. Hand rolls $5 each or 3 for $12.

name: Apapachó
source: Park Slope Walk / Park Slope Pulse / Infatuation June 2026
note: Mexican restaurant from two former Alma Negra owners — Jimmy Placido and Acencion Mendez (also ex-Fonda) — slated to open mid-June 2026 at 191 5th Ave between Berkeley Pl and Union St in Park Slope. Housemade tortillas, focus on Mexican comfort fare. Confirmed open by mid-June 2026.

name: Wild Horses
source: Eater NY / Infatuation / Cititour / Jambands June 2026
note: "Cosmic country and Western saloon" from the Niagara and Lucinda's team. Opened June 2, 2026 at 327 Bowery (street level above Bowery Palace). Mid-century decor, dark wood paneling, velvet paintings, Nudie Suits, Gram Parsons as patron saint. Live events including piano karaoke and two-step nights; Elizabeth Cook and Aaron Lee Tasjan played opening night. Open 7 days from 3pm.

name: Porta Coffee
note: Second coffee shop from the Dark Necessity team (679 Classon Ave) — sister cafe on Washington Ave between Bergen & Dean, right at the Prospect Heights / Crown Heights border. Espresso program plus pastries.

name: Cookies N' Cream
source: Park Slope Pulse / Cookies N Cream / Grubhub May 2026
note: Brooklyn outpost of the soft-baked-cookie shop chain. Opened May 23, 2026 at 685 5th Ave between 20th & 21st St in South Slope, with 23+ cookie varieties; first 75 customers on opening day got complimentary cookies.

name: Socceria
source: Greenpointers / BK Mag / Hoodline / Caper May 30 2026
note: Brooklyn's first soccer-focused sports cantina from the Taqueria Ramirez team — James Beard finalist chef Giovanni Cervantes and partner Tania Apolinar with Josh Borock and John Hilmes. Opened May 30, 2026 at the former Nura space (Norman Ave & Guernsey Pl). Tacos and Mexican cantina specialties, big screens for matches; opening day sold out, returns weekend of June 6–7 then daily for World Cup kickoff June 11.

name: Grimm Tavern
source: Crain's / Park Slope Pulse / Infatuation / Brewyork June 2026
note: First satellite from 8-year-old Brooklyn brewery Grimm Artisanal Ales. Opened June 2026 in the former Olmsted space at 659 Vanderbilt Ave. Tavern menu of classic American comfort — dry-aged steaks, burgers, tallow fries, roast chicken, pan-fried trout — with Grimm beers on tap. Adjacent 1,000 sq ft cafe sells coffee, breakfast sandwiches, biscuits, muffalettas, and canned beer to-go.

name: Dinah
source: Brooklyn Bridge Parents / Dinah IG / BK Mag May 2026
note: Modern reimagining of a 1930s Brooklyn diner in the former Atlantic Bagels space at Clinton & Baltic. Hand-rolled bagels and a rotating guest-chef pop-up program. Successfully soft-opened in late May 2026; regular service announcement imminent. Counter and booths, all-day.

name: Nowon Bushwick
source: BK Mag / Slice / Time Out May 2026
note: Jae Lee's second Nowon, opened the week of May 25, 2026 right by the Jefferson L stop. Korean-American pocha (포차) with wood-fired pizzas, the Legendary Cheeseburger that built Nowon East Village's reputation, bold comfort food, and craft cocktails. Larger and louder than the original.

name: Gosh
note: Maximalist Florence-import cocktail bar that opened May 2026 in the former Juku / Yello Bar space on Mulberry. Three levels — ground-floor lounge, clubby basement, upstairs with a confessional next to the bathroom. Gnome stools, servers in Adidas track jackets, carajillos, a bat-phone-style guest list. Absurdist excess over quiet luxury.

name: Little Pine
note: Big-format new Chinatown/Two Bridges spot specializing in Dongbei (northeastern Chinese) comfort food — cumin lamb, guo bao rou, dumplings, cold dishes. Twin entrances on Division Street and 136 East Broadway. Opened late spring 2026.

name: Kome Nori
source: Infatuation / What Now NY / Park Slope Pulse May–June 2026
note: Modern 1,400 sq ft Japanese sushi/hand-roll spot from chef Sean Kang (ex-head chef DOMODOMO, Michelin Bib Gourmand 2017–23) and operator Ken Lee. Soft-opened May 19, 2026 in the former Flatiron Steakhouse space on 5th Ave between 6th and 7th Streets. Three courses (hand roll / sushi+hand roll / signature); $79 set menu — wagyu carpaccio, miso black cod, eight pieces of sushi, dessert. À la carte yellowtail ceviche, garlic bacon fried rice. Dinner Tue–Sun.

name: Ichie
note: Crown Heights sushi/omakase spot that took over the longstanding Uotora space in spring 2026. Carries on the Japanese-style hospitality of the prior occupant with a tighter omakase-leaning menu.

name: Sadie's
note: Indoor-outdoor day-to-night Seaport destination from Seaport Entertainment Group, opened April 21, 2026 at 19 Fulton St. Two-level dining room with elevated New American comfort food; Sadie's Garden Bar billed as the city's largest outdoor bar. Live music and DJ sets on the patio.

name: Undercurrent
note: 20-seat tasting-room bar that opened in May 2026 beneath sister bar Echo Lake. Owners Chloe Frechette and Paul McGee pour rare and vintage rums plus hard-to-find spirits, including selections from their personal collection. Coral-reef-inspired design by Le Whit.

name: Drāvida
source: EV Grieve / What Now NY / Time Out May 21 2026
note: Indian-diaspora tasting room from celebrity chef Aarthi Sampath (Chopped, Beat Bobby Flay). 20-seat counter with four tables. Menu pulls from South Asian diaspora — doubles, bunny chow, blue crab curry ramen. Opened May 21, 2026. Tues–Sat 4:30–10:30pm.

name: Oriana
note: Chef Andy Quinn and sommelier Cedric Nicaise (of The Noortwyck) opened this two-floor Nolita flagship May 19, 2026, in a restored 1890s building at Mott & Broome. Open wood-fired grill menu — turbot with mussels, whole barbecue duck, grilled leeks with gigante beans and taleggio. 7,000-bottle cellar; vinyl listening lounge curated by Rosanne Cash and Rufus Wainwright.

name: Selene by Kyma
source: Haute Living / National Herald / amNY May 18 2026
note: 10,000 sq ft modern-Aegean spot inside ModernHaus Soho, opened May 18, 2026. Three-story space with a retractable-roof atrium (a nod to Selene, Greek moon goddess), terrace, and garden. Executive chef Christos Bisiotis. Homemade pita; whole grilled fish; 250–300 seats.

name: Sanwits
source: EV Grieve / Edible Manhattan / Infatuation May 2026
note: Filipino sandwich shop from the Unapologetic Foods team (Semma, Naks, Adda), led by Chef Eric Valdez of Naks. Soft-opened mid-May 2026 in the former Rowdy Rooster space. Six sandwiches — Lechon Dip with pork asado on a hero, Chicken Bagoong with eggplant aioli on brioche, Ribeye Caldereta. Tues–Sun 4–10pm.

name: Little Falcon
note: Reboot of Third Falcon as a $48 three-course prix-fixe French bistro — opened May 1, 2026. Chef Cali Faulkner (Crown Shy, Eleven Madison Park). Menu: oysters, roast chicken, chocolate mousse cake. French classics + vin de soif under $100.

name: Casabuela Café
note: Puerto Rican–Nuyorican café between Bedford and Driggs — coffee, café con leche, and cocktails in a reimagined-living-room space. Taíno coquí frog emblem; puzzles on tables in front, bar in back. Heritage-driven concept rooted in matriarchs and community.

name: Lady Moo Moo (Bushwick)
source: Brownstoner / Brooklyn Paper / Grime Square May 2026
note: Bushwick expansion of the Bed-Stuy ice cream shop (in business since 2013). Year-round Tues–Sun 10am–10pm in the former General Irving coffee space — bright blue and cow print, walk-up window, indoor seating. Full menu of scoops, sundaes, milkshakes, root beer floats. Ice cream still made at Chauncey St.

name: Dumbo Oyster Bar
note: Oyster bar under the arches of the Brooklyn Bridge. East Coast oysters, towers to share, lobster rolls, clam chowder, squid ink pasta, New York strip, Junior's cheesecake. Soft-opened through May 1, 2026; now fully open.

name: Malavita
note: Italian-leaning sandwich shop near McCarren Park. Sandos, salads, antipasti. Opened May 2026.

name: DOK Brooklyn
note: Thai restaurant & bar in the former Barbaco Tío space. Crab fried rice, khao soi, kai yang (grilled chicken with spicy tamarind), drinking snacks. Recently soft-opened.

name: The Cat Bar
note: Neighborhood bar featuring local jazz and folk musicians. Soft-opened May 2026 on the south edge of Park Slope/Windsor Terrace.

name: Taikii Cafe
note: Vinyl cafe serving coffee, matcha, inari, and vegan pastries. Plays records on a built-in vinyl rig. Opened May 2026.

name: Sendo
note: Budget-friendly omakase counter from chef Tsuyoshi Takahashi (formerly Sushi Noz). Edo-era-style nigiri and handrolls, omakase sets around $34. Opened May 2026.

name: Thick & Thin Pizza
note: All-halal Italian restaurant with New York- and Detroit-style pies on sourdough. Also serves pizza sandwiches, pastas, lasagna, meatballs, and tiramisu. Opened April 2026.

name: Moonburger
note: Hudson Valley burger chain opened inside Olly Olly Market on April 9, 2026. Third NYC location. Adopted meat from small farms in NY.

name: GangJong Thunpa Phunshi Restaurant
note: Tibetan street foods including momo dumplings, cold laphing, mapo tofu, hand-pulled noodle soups, butter tea. Opened April 8, 2026.

name: Sosa's Sandwich Shop
note: Cuban sandwiches, arepas, monthly specials inspired by customer stories. Opened April 2026.

name: Greenpoint Comedy Club
note: Entertainment venue with ambitious beverage program. Local beers, biodynamic wines, vermouth, sake. Comedy, live music, movie screenings, burlesque. Opened April 10, 2026.

name: COTE 550
note: Second NYC location of Korean steakhouse. More mature version of Cote with upgraded Butcher's Feast. Housed in Philip Johnson-designed skyscraper at 550 Madison. Opened April 18, 2026.

name: Marcel
note: French restaurant in Sotheby's Breuer building. Partnership between Sotheby's and Roman and Williams. Continental menu with French emphasis, duck confit, côte de boeuf. Dining room as art gallery with works by Warhol, Joan Mitchell, Calder. Opened April 16, 2026.

name: Al Volo
note: Contemporary Italian at Market 57 at Pier 57. Aperitivo culture, panini, pizza al taglio, focacce. Opened April 24, 2026.

name: Skinny Louie
note: Miami-born smash burger concept. Third NYC location. Fresh Black Angus beef burgers. Opened April 24, 2026.

name: Motek
note: Second NYC location of Miami-born modern Mediterranean restaurant. Housemade laffa, shawarma, schnitzel. April 2026.

name: Pure Green
note: Acai and smoothie chain. New UWS location. Opened April 17, 2026.

name: Ugly Baby
note: Reopened April 2026 in Williamsburg after Carroll Gardens closure. Larger space, boundary-pushing Thai with real heat.

name: Fellini Coffee
note: Sixth location of lavish latte cafe chain. Tiramisu and banana split flavors. Opened April 17, 2026.

name: Andiamo
note: Sailing-themed wine and martini bar. Nautical theme, 7am opening, martini bar. Opened April 22, 2026.

name: Voco Roofbar
note: Rooftop bar and restaurant on 33rd floor of Voco Times Square. Tapas-style menu, retractable roof. Opened April 22, 2026.

name: The Consulate
note: French American from Consulate team. Raw bar, short rib cavatelli, steaks. Opened April 17, 2026 with At the Office bar.

name: Morgenstern's
note: Reopened April 20, 2026 in original 2014 space. Contemporary ice cream with experimental flavors. Noon to midnight.

name: YEERO Greek Eatery
note: Fast-casual Mediterranean. Salads, bowls, pitas, gyros, Greek comfort foods. 10-seat spot. Opened April 18, 2026.

name: WatchHouse
address: 287 Park Ave S, New York, NY 10010
note: UK coffee shop chain. Third NYC location. Midcentury Madison Avenue aesthetic. Opened mid-April 2026.

name: Myka
note: Frozen Greek yogurt with toppings. International chain debut. Opened April 18, 2026.

name: Cleo
note: From Margot and Montague Diner team. Rotisserie chicken marinated 24 hours in Lebanese seven spice. Opened April 17, 2026.

name: Fireboat
address: Pier 6, Brooklyn Bridge Park, Brooklyn, NY 11201
note: From Grand Banks team. Decommissioned FDNY fireboat serving Caribbean-inspired bites and frozen cocktails from ship's original fire cannons. Seasonal, opened Spring 2026 at Pier 6 (former Pilot location).

name: Arthur
note: Neighborhood bistro in Greenpoint. Refined bistro classics with chef's counter. Opened April 2026.

name: Dean's
note: From King restaurant team. Pub focused on British seafood named after Suffolk fisherman Dean Fryer. Opened March 2026.

name: Double Knot NYC
note: From Philadelphia chef Michael Schulson. Sushi restaurant and robatayaki spot. Opened February 2026.

name: Or'esh
note: From Catch Hospitality Group with chef Nadav Greenberg. Mediterranean with wood- and coal-fired grill. Opened February 2026.

name: Echo Lake
note: From Chloe Frechette (Punch) and Paul McGee (Lost Lake). $12 daily daiquiris, 'Tiny and a Tot' lager/rum shot combo. Seafood plates: oysters with tepache mignonette, green coconut curry chowder.

name: Gigi's
note: From Fulgurances Laundromat team. Pink neon-lit rotisserie chicken spot with European wine program. Chefs Thomas Knodell and Émile Desme (Le Chateaubriand, Paris). Will revive culinary incubator program.

name: Ms. Alice
note: From Hole In The Wall team. European aperitivo-inspired cocktail bar. Daily 'apero hour' 4-6pm with $12 negronis, spritzes, martinis. Italian bar snacks like gnocco fritto.

name: Oyatte
note: 30-seat tasting menu restaurant from chef Hasung Lee (Atomix, The French Laundry, Geranium). One of spring's most anticipated openings. Reservation-only.

name: Peek Inn
note: From Cozy Royale and The Meat Hook team. Guinness pours, loaded hot dogs (incl. smile-shaped), Hudson Valley meats. No-frills hangout with $10 happy hour deals (4-7pm) and $5 beer-and-shot combos.

name: Yesterday's
note: Old-school New York vibe on Bleecker Street. Classic neighborhood bar feel like it's been there for decades. Hours: Mon-Wed 4pm-midnight, Thu 4pm-2am, Fri noon-4am.

name: GiGi Curry & Noodle Bar
note: Quick-service Thai noodle shop. Added to Eater Brooklyn heatmap March 2026. Bushwick only — included for exceptional quality.

name: Il Leone
note: Cult Peaks Island pizzamaker Ben Wexler-Waite's NYC return. Naturally leavened, Neapolitan-style. Maine/Italian ingredients, lobster 'Isola' pie. Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Mariscos El Submarino
note: 5th location. Walk-in only. Aguachiles, el gobernador tacos, tostadas with buttery tuna. Infatuation 8.2 rating. Brooklyn hit list.

name: Tamalería La Madrina
note: From Taqueria Milear team. Tight menu: antojitos, taquitos, tamales. Oaxaqueño w/ pork in banana leaf is standout. Infatuation Brooklyn hit list.

name: Vato
note: All-day from Corima chef Fidel Caballero. Tortillas, breakfast burritos, conchas. El Paso/Ciudad Juárez heritage. Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Border Town
note: Cult pop-up gone brick-and-mortar. Tortilla master Jorge Aguilar's Northern Mexican. Flour tortillas, tacos de guisados. Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Diljān Bakery
note: Afghan naan, halwa sticky buns, sheer pira pastries. From Little Flower Cafe team + baker Bryan Ford. Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Kelang
note: From Hainan Chicken House (Sunset Park). Oxtail rendang w/ Haitian-Malaysian rice. Caribbean-Southeast Asian fusion. Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Sea & Soil
note: Worker-owned coop. Relocated from Cobble Hill. Smoked trout sandwiches, Sunday pizza. Sliding scale pricing. Infatuation just-added.

name: Bistrot Ha
note: Follow-up to Ha's Snack Bar. Weekly rotating menu. Crab soup w/ calf's brain, oyster martini, vol-au-vents. CN Traveler pick March 2026.

name: Ramblin' Chick
note: From Ample Hills founders. Fried chicken, burgers. Court St. Grub Street mention April 2026.

name: Alma Negra
note: Polished Mexican — mezcal-focused bar program

name: Barker Cafeteria
note: Daytime spot. Focaccia sandwiches, roast beef w/ horseradish, chicken noodle soup, apple fritters. The 'Dad Plate.' Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Cafe Mado
note: New American-French. NYT Brooklyn best 2025

name: Confidant
note: Relocated to Brooklyn Heights. New American. Added to Eater Brooklyn heatmap March 2026.

name: DOLORES
note: Comida chilanga. $4 papadillas — masa stuffed with potato, fried, two salsas

name: Entre Nous
note: Natural wine bar from Fradei Bistro team. Oeuf mayo, leeks vinaigrette, tartiflette croquettes. French apéro tradition

name: Evelina
note: Italian. Consistently mentioned among Fort Greene best

name: FOLK
note: From Jay Kumar of Lore (Bib Gourmand). Crab risotto, biryani arancini, masala latkes

name: GERTIE
note: Reimagined Jewish deli from Gertrude's team. On-premise bagel baking, appetizing shop meets deli. 602 Vanderbilt

name: Kabawa
note: $145 prix-fixe Caribbean. Jerk sausage with chicken liver, rice, braised duck leg

name: La Fleur Rouge
note: Chinese-French from a Michelin-trained chef

name: Lei
note: Annie Shi's wine bar. Shanxi-style mao er duo — lamb/tomato ragu cat's ear pasta with dill

name: Leland Eating and Drinking House
source: BK Mag, Resy Hit List Mar 2026
note: Mediterranean + Chinese/Filipino. Focaccia, smoked pollock fritters, chicken jook, whole fried black sea bass

name: Miss Ada
note: Israeli/Middle Eastern

name: Sailor
note: American-Mediterranean. NYT Brooklyn best list 2025

name: Sofreh
note: Persian. NYT Brooklyn best 2025

name: Third Time's the Charm
note: From Bad Cholesterol pizza cult. Wood-fired pies, chicken nuggets w/ gochujang, blooming onion, cocktails. Backyard. Eater Brooklyn heatmap.

name: Zimmi's
note: Provençal. Fried pumpkin with fennel, honey, pepper

name: Café Fleuri
note: Southern France + North African touches. In old Schilling space. Chef drama (Baumgart left). Poached haddock, lamb shoulder, rice pudding.

name: Cocktail Omakase
note: From Superbueno/Katana Kitten team. Tokyo-inspired 4-cocktail + 4-bite omakase ($55). Menu changes biweekly. Walk-in Bar 7 next door.

name: Falansai
note: Moved from Bushwick. All-day format. Duck-neck tamales, pho-spiced lamb, $58 tasting. Grub Street April highlight.

name: Soba Ulala
note: From Hirohisa chef. House-made soba twice daily w/ buckwheat from upstate NY. Sashimi, tamago sides. Eater April spotlight.

name: Sono
note: Korean-Italian trattoria. Housemade soju, handmade noodles, bottarga pasta with nori. Now open.

name: Bar Chucho
note: From Corima team. Cantina bar with cocktails, micheladas, queso chihuahua burger. Opened April 2, 2026.

name: Beto's Carnitas & Guisados
address: 69 Clinton St Store front A, New York, NY 10002
note: Daily carnitas in steel cauldron. Guisados, gorditas with heirloom Masienda corn. Grub Street pick.

name: Cô Lạc
note: Chef Helen Nguyen (Saigon Social). $55 set menu, bánh dumplings, dry-aged duck. Grub Street April pick.

name: Fat Ronnie's Burger Bar
note: Martha's Vineyard import. Thick patties, fish sandwiches, chopped cheese. Harlem family legacy.

name: Honey Badger
note: Wild-to-table from a Noma fermentation lab alum. Foraged and fermented ingredients.

name: L'Industrie Pizzeria
note: Third location of beloved Williamsburg slice shop. Standing room only. Opened March 19.

name: Malvan
note: Michelin-starred Chintan Pandya (Dhamaka, Semma). Coastal Indian seafood focus.

name: Strange Delight
note: Modern oyster bar near BAM. Brooklyn-New Orleans vibe, great fried shrimp, cocktails. Jim's pick.

name: TBD Gimbap
note: Nami Nori team pop-up. Lines forming before open sign flips. Takeout gimbap ~$12. Runs through May+.

name: Trudie's Tavern
note: From the Gertrude's team. American bar and grill.

name: Golden Steer at One Fifth
note: NYC expansion of iconic 1958 Las Vegas steakhouse. Tuxedoed servers, wet-aged USDA Prime steaks, Art Deco setting in landmark One Fifth Avenue building. Opened January 2026.

name: Odo East Village
note: Casual offshoot of two-Michelin-star Odo. Chef Hiroki Odo's 'kaiseki izakaya' concept — refined kaiseki technique in relaxed izakaya setting. À la carte, 24 seats, fully gluten-free. Opened February 2026.

name: Stars
note: Pocket-sized 12-seat wine bar from the team behind Claud and Penny. 1,000+ bottle list with 16 by the glass, plus small snacks like chicken liver mousse and blinis with ham and maple butter. Walk-in only. Opened December 2025.

name: What The Falafel
note: Tiny all-vegetarian (mostly vegan) Middle Eastern counter. Pita and falafel baked fresh daily in a gas oven; pitas and bowls with tahini, hummus, and baba ghanoush. About three tables, casual.

name: Bar Ferdinando
note: Italian bar and cafe reviving 121-year-old Ferdinando's Focacceria. From Sal Lamboglia (Cafe Spaghetti, Swoony's). Opened April 15, 2026.

name: Lithos Mediterranean Cuisine
note: Modern Greek restaurant with Mykonos energy in FiDi. Seafood orzo, grilled lamb chops, Mediterranean sea bass. Opened April 1, 2026.

name: Pies 'n' Thighs
source: Brooklyn Eagle / Hellgate / FSR — Pies 'n' Thighs opens 2nd location (Apr 2026)
note: Park Slope outpost of the 20-year-old Williamsburg fried-chicken institution from Sarah Sanneh. Opened April 1, 2026 at the corner of Flatbush and 6th Ave in the former Pecking House space. Counter-service with fried chicken, biscuit sandwiches, pies, new chicken tenders, plus shareable sides. Family-friendly.

name: After Eden | Cocktail & Coffee Bar
note: Vietnamese coffee shop by day, bar by night. Jackfruit lattes, cà phê muối, pho-tini cocktails. Opened April 10, 2026.

name: Whits
address: 34 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10003
note: Late-night slider joint with onion rings, fries, ripper hot dogs, Pat Lafrieda patties. Opened April 9, 2026.

name: One40 Rooftop Restaurant & Bar
note: 22nd-floor rooftop bar at One40 Rooftop with downtown skyline views. Golden Hour outdoor extension opened April 15, 2026.

name: Two Tigers
note: Sleek wine bar in former Screamer's Pizzeria space. Curated wines plus small bites: house Boursin cheese, fries with tarragon béarnaise. Opened March 2026.

name: Le Fournil
note: Third location of French bakery (originally East Village, also UES). Authentic baguettes and specialty pastries. Opened March 2026.

name: Slope Cheesesteak and Shakes
note: Cheesesteaks, loaded fries, milkshakes, chicken wings. Opened March 2026.

name: Dial Up Days
note: 1990s-themed snack shop and milk tea cafe. Opened March 2026.

name: Tenon Vegan Sushi
note: All-vegan sushi from Toronto-based concept; first Brooklyn location. Opened March 2026.

name: Canyon Coffee
note: LA-based cafe expanding to Brooklyn. Streamlined espresso program and bright toasts. Opened March 2026.

name: Cicchetti BK
note: Venetian wine bar pouring 15 wines by the glass at $14 each. Cocktails and small bites: crostini with porchetta, broccolini salsa verde. Opened March 2026.

name: Hungry Thirsty
note: Successor to Ugly Baby Thai run by former staff. Northern Thai street food, intensely spiced dishes, family recipes. Opened March 2026.

name: Lundy's of Brooklyn
note: Revived Sheepshead Bay seafood institution from Sandra Nicholas and Mark Snyder. Burgers, broiled lobster, deviled eggs with crab, clam bisque. Opened in former Rocky Sullivan's space, March 2026.

name: Lincoln Station Park Slope
note: All-day cafe by the Al Di La (Bar Corvo) team. Rotisserie chicken sandwiches, meatballs, kids menu. Sister to Prospect Heights original. Opened January 2026.

name: BADABOOM
note: French rotisserie and comfort classics from Henry's Wine and Spirits / Sunrise/Sunset team. Steak frites, fish, seasonal vegetables, French wine program.

name: moco 575
note: Coffee shop specializing in Yemeni and Peruvian coffee. Also pastries, sandwiches, brunch.

name: The Office Brooklyn
note: Reservation-only zero-proof speakeasy inside a residential building. Mocktails and coffee.

name: Joys' Cafe
note: Hong Kong-style cha chaan teng with takeout counter and 70-seat dining room. ~80 items: condensed milk French toast, baked pork chops with spaghetti, HK milk tea, pineapple buns. All under $15.

name: Phê
note: Bánh mì cafe adjacent to sister restaurant Mắm. Vietnamese coffee and sandwich service. Opened Spring 2026.

name: Brookiez
note: Bakery specializing in brownie-cookie hybrids in flavors like cheesecake, blondie, and Oreo. Also lattes and gelato. Opened April 2026.

name: Toby's Estate Coffee
note: Australian coffee roaster's Tribeca cafe with checkered floors, cane-backed chairs, hi-fi system. Opened April 2026.

name: Cafe Susanne
note: Bright, art-centric all-day restaurant and bar in Domino Park. Coffee, pastries, sandwiches by day; craft beer, wine, cocktails, snacks at night. Sister Bar Susanne (raw bar) opened later in spring 2026.

name: SUD NYC
note: Italian restaurant featuring 'irregular' pizza (between oval and rectangle). Italian-trained chef; planning monthly cooking classes.

name: Lamonte Pizza
note: Pizzeria from Lamonte bistro team across the street. Classic NY-style and grandma pies.

name: Bodega Nights
note: Brazilian-influenced restaurant from the Babysips (LES) team. Concise wine program.

name: Kitaro
note: Japanese-inspired cafe with coffee, matcha, hojicha, and homemade onigiri (mentaiko, yuzu kosho chicken).

name: konya NYC
note: From the Domodomo and Konban team. Specializes in katsu and noodle dishes. Opened April 2026.

name: Drums & Flats
source: What Now NY / Park Slope Pulse May 2026
note: Wing-focused bar from the Moot Bar / Uncle Barry's team. Six wing flavors (buffalo, lemon herb, pok-pok), fried cheese curds, burgers, sandwiches. Opened May 2, 2026 in former Alchemy space.

name: Bar Bruno
note: Reboot under the Greenpoint Fish & Lobster team (Peter Juusola, chef Orion Russell, Vinny Milburn). Half chicken under mole, broiled sea bass, signature tacos. Reopened spring 2026.

name: Richie's
note: Neighborhood bar in former Catfish space. $14 cocktails, $12 wine glasses, $5 Miller High Lifes, weekday 3-6pm happy hour, popcorn, big TVs. Opened spring 2026.

name: Candor Candy
note: First gummy candy and pick-and-mix sweets shop in this part of Brooklyn. Female-founded by Cat Cirino with a high-design space and donations to NYC food banks. Opened April 2026.

name: Nacha Focaccia
note: Vegan cafe and bakery specializing in focaccia with creative flavors (PB&J among them), focaccia sandwiches, and specialty drinks. Opened spring 2026.

name: Forever Brewing
source: Brooklyn Paper / Park Slope Pulse April 2026
note: Forever Homebrewing founders Matt Land and Rob Jenkins took over the former Strong Rope taproom. Homebrew supplies up front, small-batch beers, rare styles, NA options, planned coffee program. Soft-opened April 11, full menu rolling out in May.

name: Andrew Steak Society
note: Steakhouse from the Aquarelle team, red velvet seating, wood-fired grill, 28-day dry-aged beef. Chef Niklas Lucich. Opened April 2026.

name: Dulce
note: Dominican sit-down sibling to El Sazón R.D. Braised goat tacos, whole red snapper, Dominican classics. Opened spring 2026.

name: Chama Mama
note: Authentic Georgian cuisine — khachapuri, pkhali (walnut-veggie spreads), Georgian dumplings, hearty stews. Lunch/dinner Mon–Fri, weekend brunch. Opened April 2026.

name: Grabstein's Bagels
note: "Old school bagels with a new school vibe." Hand-rolled bagels, house-made spreads, creative breakfast sandwiches. Brooklyn outpost of the growing NYC mini-chain. Opened April 2026.

name: Pho Rainbow
note: Vietnamese soft-opened on the Bergen/Dean stretch of Smith St. Fresh pho, banh mi, bubble tea, rice plates, noodle soups in a casual setting. Opened April 2026.

name: Kasbah Bakery
note: Moroccan bakery on the Columbia Waterfront — fresh pastries crafted with traditional techniques and authentic ingredients, plus sandwiches in a small café space. Opened April 2026.

name: 100 Feast & Lounge
note: Chinatown's newest Cantonese & dim sum restaurant on the 2nd & 3rd floors at 100 East Broadway. Opened Winter/Spring 2026.

name: Bao Noodle House
note: Specializes in bao buns and noodles. New Chinatown spot on the Bowery. Opened Winter/Spring 2026.

name: Me and Paul's
note: Reopened the original Paulie Gee's space at 60 Greenpoint Ave on April 30, 2026 — same wood-fired pizza recipes, but reformatted as a tavern with a longer bar, booths, pool table, darts and a jukebox. Order at the bar.

name: Egg & Soy
note: Small Taiwanese breakfast/all-day spot in the former Partea XP space. Fàntuán, dànbǐng, luóbo gāo, egg & cucumber sandwiches, soy milk and pastries. Soft-opened spring 2026; now in regular service per Infatuation and Yelp reviews.

name: Nori Shinn
note: Japanese spot on 1st Ave in the East Village, listed among April 2026 NYC openings on The Infatuation's running new-openings guide.

name: Kings Co Imperial
source: Park Slope Pulse / Threads — Park Slope Living (May 2026)
note: Williamsburg Chinese-American favorite expanded to Park Slope, taking over the former Stone Park Cafe events space at 357 3rd St near 5th Ave. Soy sauce on tap, wok-fired classics, long dumplings, mock eel, Kung Pao chicken, prawn fries.

name: Much Obliged
note: Alphabet City bar from the team behind Greenpoint's Gator. Fried fish sandwich, Corsican wine, banana-infused espresso martini, Cynar paloma.

name: Lazy Bulldog
note: Turkish-style coffee shop graduated from a 6th Street stoop cart to a brick-and-mortar. Coffee brewed traditionally over heated sand; flavors include lavender, cardamom, ginger, and chili, plus spiced chai and small baked items.

name: Lucky Cafe
source: EV Grieve — Openings: Lucky Cafe on Avenue A (Apr 2026)
note: Coffee and matcha shop on Avenue A from East Village locals James Phillips and barista Max Vitkus. Pastries from Brooklyn's Colson Patisserie. Open daily 8am–6pm.

name: Silk Noodle Bar
note: Specialist for luosifen — Liuzhou river-snail rice noodle soup from Guangxi — bold, sour, spicy, with fermented bamboo shoots. Bowls $14–$20. Often packed; expect a wait.

name: Wallace
note: Neighborhood wine bar on Vanderbilt Ave with backyard seating, an extensive by-the-glass program, and a small food menu featuring gildas, oysters, and martinis. Just walking distance from Prospect Park.

name: Tavern Next Door
source: The Infatuation — Tavern Next Door review (May 2026)
note: Walk-in cocktail bar from the Red Hook Tavern team, located right next door. Small food menu including a version of the famous dry-aged tavern burger. Reservation-friendly overflow option for the perpetually booked flagship.

name: Kinda Nice
note: Cocktail bar that opened May 7, 2026 in the former Black Flamingo space. Owners Patty Dennison and Ally Marrone (ex-bartenders) lean into nostalgic Americana — Guava Negroni, Greek Salad Martini, banana Old Fashioned, deviled eggs, grilled cheese. Open daily 5pm–midnight.

name: Café Bar J.F.
source: Greenpointers / Resy — Café Bar J.F. opens (May 13, 2026)
note: South American tavern that opened May 13, 2026 in the former Llama Inn space. From Llama Group's Juan Correa with Chef Francisco Castillo (ex-Llama San). Nostalgia-driven menu drawing on Peru, Chile, and Argentina; South American cocktails from Sarah Morrissey; wine program by Pierre Buffet. Dinner Wed–Sun.

name: Zoli
source: Greenpointers / The Infatuation — Zoli opens at Amant (May 2026)
note: Seafood-forward American restaurant from chef Ned Baldwin (Houseman) that opened in early May 2026 inside the Amant arts foundation. 35-seat dining room and 18-seat bar separated by Pierre Huyghe's 'Satellite' aquarium triptych. Menu flips the usual ratio — fish leads, other proteins secondary.

name: Lonnies
source: Cobble Hill Collective / Resy / Infatuation — Lonnies opens (May 20, 2026)
note: Diner-inspired neighborhood spot from Sean Rembold and Caron Callahan (Ingas Bar) that opened May 20, 2026 at 112 Bond at Pacific. Lo-fi, approachable menu: daily prime rib, chicken under a brick, burger with locally butchered beef. Cozy classic-diner aesthetic. Named for Lonnie Lanham — Kentucky Vietnam vet turned hairdresser from Rembold's childhood.

name: Bar Susanne
source: Greenpointers / BK Mag / Infatuation — Bar Susanne opens at Domino Park (May 15, 2026)
note: Seafood-and-raw-bar cocktail spot that opened May 15, 2026 next to Cafe Susanne on the Williamsburg waterfront. Chef Jackie Carnesi (ex-Kellogg's Diner, Nura) leading the kitchen with North Fork ingredients — shrimp cocktail, scallop crudo, crab salad, grilled squid, fluke Milanese, roasted chicken. Coastal-inspired cocktail program from Milos Zica. Designed by JBA-nominee Matthew Maddy. Open 5–11pm daily.

name: Six Coasts by Smorgasburg
source: Average Socialite / I Love NY / Time Out — Six Coasts grand opening (May 9, 2026)
note: Smorgasburg's first full-scale restaurant — 32,000-sqft waterfront venue that opened May 9, 2026 on Soissons Landing in the former Island Oyster space. Pan-American seafood from Chef Scotley Innis (Jamaican-American) spanning Nova Scotia, Gulf Coast, Baja, Bahia. Tropical cocktails, sunset DJ series, visiting-chef residencies, harbor views.

name: Gusi
source: Infatuation / Resy / What Now NY — Gusi opens (May 8, 2026)
note: Modern Eastern European two-story restaurant that opened May 8, 2026 across from Jefferson Market Library. From husband-wife team Boris Artemyev and Elena Melnikova. Puff-pastry pierogi; pelmeni with beef/pork, veal, elk, buffalo, goose, or tahini-finished Mediterranean version; five weekly-rotating borscht. Elegant ground-floor bar plus intimate upstairs dining room.

name: Lalaon
source: Infatuation / Time Out / The Most Anticipated Restaurants Opening in Brooklyn May 2026
note: Two-story Korean BBQ on the Williamsburg waterfront from Ju Young Oh and Min Sung Kang of studiOH Hospitality (Atti, Kuun, Ms. OHHO). Largest Korean BBQ in Brooklyn — house-aged meats, traditional Korean dishes, makgeolli and soju program, large outdoor patio with East River views toward Smorgasburg.

name: Birdee Botequim
note: Evening counterpart to chef Renata Ameni's daytime Brazilian bakery Birdee, opened May 23. Shareable São Paulo-inspired small bites, cocktails like the Pessegro (Aperol, Japanese plum) and Tropicale (sherry, pineapple, green tea), wine program from Kent Hospitality's Kristen Goceljak. Open Thu–Sat 5–9pm. Ameni was a 2026 James Beard semifinalist for Outstanding Pastry Chef.

name: Last Crumb
note: First brick-and-mortar from viral LA-born DTC cookie brand Last Crumb (founded 2020 by Derek Jaeger), opened May 28 between Bedford and Berry. Single cookies $8.50, 3-count box $24, 6-count $45. Coffee from Parlor plus the brand's own Shaken Milk. Daily 11am–10pm; first 100 visitors Thu–Sat get a free cookie.

name: Soft Hours
note: Song Dynasty-inspired tea house from artist Juno Shen — a deliberately quiet sanctuary built around seasonal tea service, small confections, incense, floral arrangements, and rotating art. Philosophy rooted in the Song-era ritual of pausing with tea, flowers, and art as part of daily life.

name: Love Thy Neighbor
source: Time Out / The Infatuation / Spirits Business May 2026
note: All-day Japanese-inspired bar from Shigefumi Kabashima (ROKC, NR) and chef Elias Popa (Oti) in the basement that was legendary jazz club 55 Bar until 2022. Food from Takanori Akiyama (ex-Tsukimi) — Japanese tapas, sandos, share plates. Signature cocktails: Tokyo Banana (banana-infused whisky, vermouth, milk-brew coffee, shio koji) and a wasabi-infused G&T. A queer-celebratory space — a brick from the Stonewall Inn and a Marsha P. Johnson quote set into the floor.

name: Allegretto al Forno
source: Eat Up NY / Infatuation / Greenpointers May 2026
note: Casual wood-fired pizza & Southern Italian small-plates spinoff of Michelin-starred Francie next door, opened May 2026. From chef Chris Cipollone and GM John Winterman. Signature duck-sausage pizza with apricot and pistachio pesto; sfizi-driven menu built around the wood oven.

name: Prince Street Pizza
note: Brooklyn debut of the Nolita pepperoni-square institution, opened on Smith St in spring 2026. Cult-favorite spicy square slices and easy grab-and-go counter service in Carroll Gardens.

name: Pizza Studio Tamaki
address: 123 St Marks Pl, New York, NY 10009
source: Eat Up NY / Infatuation / Hoodline May 2026
note: First US outpost of Tokyo pizzaiolo Tsubasa Tamaki's celebrated PST, opened May 5, 2026 in the former Moody Tongue space. Tokyo-Neapolitan style with a proprietary Japanese flour blend, deep-char crust, and 30-hour fermented dough. Bismarck with egg ($36), Bianca with white truffle oil.

name: Le Sixth
note: Day-to-night West Village French bistro from a French chef with Hong Kong fine-dining pedigree, opened May 2026. Sole Grenobloise and steak frites at dinner; French omelets, benedicts, and chicken paillard on the weekend brunch menu.

name: Somssi
source: WWD / Resy / Culinary Agents / WhatNow June 2026
note: Debut concept from Ahris Kim of NA:EUN Hospitality — the team behind Atoboy, Atomix and Naro. A personality-driven 50-seat bistro serving American-by-way-of-Korea fare with shareable plates drawing on Asian and European ingredients, techniques and immigrant nostalgia. Backed by Ellia and Junghyun 'JP' Park; opened early June 2026 in the South Village with a full bar.

name: Edoya Sushi
note: Family-run Edomae-style omakase counter from Chef Edgar and his son in the former Sushi Lin space. Seasonal sushi built around traditional Japanese technique with both omakase counter and table seatings; soft-opened spring 2026 and recently added to Resy.

name: BKB Brooklyn Brasserie
source: Brownstoner / BKB site / Brooklyn Paper June 2026
note: Family-friendly Belgian brasserie on the corner of Stuyvesant and Macon, taking over the former Mama Fox space. Moules frites, steak frites, waffles, bitterballen and beef bourguignon plus ~10 mostly Belgian beer lines, French wines and multiple TVs for soccer matches. Soft-opened in late May 2026 with a full debut targeted for June 20.

name: Variety Coffee Roasters
source: Greenpointers May 22 2026 / WhatNow / Coffee Klatsch June 2026
note: Brooklyn-based Variety Coffee Roasters opened its 10th cafe — and largest, most design-forward to date — on June 1, 2026 at The Astral building in Greenpoint, taking over a former laundromat between Java and India Streets. Drip and espresso from their Bushwick roastery alongside pastries from Colson Patisserie, Ole & Steen, and The Good Batch.

name: Phil's Place
note: Reincarnation of beloved Graham Ave dive Jimbo Slims after a ownership change and revamp. Mainstay Jimbo's bartender Phil Shepherd partnered with Pomp and Circumstance chef James Ahearn for a renovated room and a new bar-snacks menu (chicken meatball Caesar heros) — drinks stayed in the friendly $9 shot-and-beer, $12-15 cocktails range.

name: Athena Keke's
note: Brooklyn's first dedicated queer women's sports bar from couple Claudia Capriles and Alexandra Murray, who graduated from years of pop-ups into a brick-and-mortar at 222 Greene Ave in Clinton Hill. Lots of screens for women's sports, a full kitchen drawing on Murray's Baltimore roots and Capriles's Bolivian upbringing, and a library corner of queer-history and women's-sports books.

name: Olmo
source: Brooklyn Eagle / Brownstoner / The Infatuation / Hoodline 2026
note: Mexico City-style 'cantina Neoyorquina' from Cosme and Pujol alums, with chef Jael Lopez. 850 sq ft corner spot at Saratoga & Decatur featuring a starry-night mural by Alannah Farrell with jacaranda and elm trees. Cheesy queso fundido, chicharron preparado, agave cocktails, beer-based micheladas. Wed-Sun 5-11pm, plates $10-25, drinks ~$15.

name: Selune
source: Brooklyn Paper / Time Out / Cititour / Patch June 2026
note: Oyster and natural wine bar from Marc Lioussanne (ex-Eden Rock St. Barths, owner of Bistro Général & Mabel in Paris) and partner Bilen Gaga. Opened in a 100-year-old landmark building at Halsey & Lewis with exposed brick, a hand-stained walnut bar salvaged from prior tenants, sage chairs and traditional Ethiopian art. 120+ natural wines (heavy France), oysters, light seafood, charcuterie. Mon-Sat 5pm-midnight (1am Fri-Sat).

name: Tsuki
note: Neo-Japanese tasting room that opened June 3, 2026 on 7th Ave between 21st and 22nd Streets. Six-course menu structured around the phases of the moon — quietly themed, seasonally driven and a step away from straight omakase.

name: Conall's Public House
note: Irish cocktail sports pub that opened May 14, 2026 on First Ave between 3rd and 4th Streets. Curry chips, bangers and mash, and a full Guinness program in a room built for watching sports — leaning into the East Village's growing Irish-pub revival.

name: Paulie Gee's Slice Shop (East Village)
note: Second slice shop from Paulie Gee's, run by longtime employee Justin Vermillion. Opened May 28, 2026 at First Ave & 6th St. Square Detroit-leaning slices, the signature Hellboy with hot honey, and the same vegan-pepperoni offerings that made the Greenpoint original a destination.

name: Tacos Domingo
note: Mexico City taqueria import from Tacos Domingo, known for carne asada. Opened June 7 at St. Marks Place (former Brooklyn Dumpling Shop). Menu: carne asada tacos, tacos piratas with melted cheese.

name: Forno D'Oro
note: Roman-style pizza from chef Salvatore Olivella. Opened June 1. Toppings: nduja, soppressata, hot honey, burrata, arugula, bresaola. At West 20th Street.

name: Ngon Vietnamese Kitchen
note: First NYC outpost of acclaimed Dallas restaurant (Michelin Bib Gourmand). Northern Vietnamese cuisine. Opened June 1 at East Fifth Street.

name: Alma Mexicana Restaurante
address: 242 East Third Street, New York, NY 10009
note: Affordable taqueria by owner Luis Cano. Opened June 1 in former Rossy's Bakery space. Tacos 2 for $4.99, quesadillas, burritos, pollo guisado, empanadas. Between Avenues B and C.

name: Wolf Pub Bar
note: Neon-lit cocktail bar with 1990s aesthetic. Opened June 5. Cassette tapes, group shot-skis, bright blue drinks in fish-shaped glassware. Near Bedford Avenue.

name: Cafe Bar J.F.
note: South American-leaning tavern from Llama Inn restaurateur Juan Correa and chef Francisco Castillo (initials = J+F). Opened May 13, 2026 in the former Llama Inn space at Withers & Meeker. Menu spans Peru, Chile, Argentina: grilled swordfish with yellow-eyed beans, lobster arroz meloso, pisco sour from Correa family recipe. Wines from Valle de Uco, Pisco Valley, Valle de Itata. Design by Madrid studio Plantea Estudio.

name: Balera
note: Two-floor Williamsburg pizzeria with a disco ball and menu rooted in Piedmont and Emilia-Romagna. Opened April 2026. Thin-crust Roman-style pizza, pastas, and a seafood salad with octopus, shrimp, and potatoes. Live music or DJs on Friday and Saturday evenings. Named after Italian dance halls (balera).

name: Eleanor's Jour Et Nuit
note: Hot new spot from Greek hospitality group Avra; name means "day and night." Sibling of piano bar Le Bar Penelope. Opened June 9, 2026 at W 33rd Street. Daytime cafe setup morphs into cocktails, dinner, and DJs at night. Avra is known for energetic, buzzy venues.

name: Cocotazo
note: Chef Frances Roman’s Puerto Rican pop-up (formerly at Queens Night Market and La Marqueta) found its permanent home in El Barrio in June 2026. Slow-roasted pernil, house-made condensed milk ice cream, bread pudding, fried cod fish fritters, guava barbecue ribs, other family recipes. At First Avenue near E 109th Street.

name: Capitaine
source: Eater NY / Infatuation / Andrea Strong / What Now NY June 2026
note: European-inspired seafood tavern from Cody Pruitt and Jacob Cohen (Libertine, Chateau Royale), chef Mike Gutterman, in the former Libertine space at Greenwich & Christopher. Opened June 19, 2026. Raw bar (East Coast oysters, razor clams, shrimp cocktail, Jonah crab claws), lobster roll, steak frites, Wagyu burger, and a throwback caviar sandwich on Japanese milk bread with sauce gribiche. Freezer-cold martinis; sapele wood paneling. Evenings + weekend brunch to start.

name: Titán
note: Fine Mexican from Alan Delgado (Los Burritos Juarez) and Ivy Mix (Leyenda, Whoopsie Daisy). Opened June 17, 2026 in DUMBO. Carne asada tacos, cod in masa tempura, aguachiles; bar leans into regional Mexican spirits like charanda and Oaxacan aguardientes with seasonal ingredients. Wed-Sun 5-10pm.

name: Unnecessary
note: Cocktail-inspired soft-serve shop from the Double Chicken Please team, which reimagines cocktails and beverages as ice cream, including alcoholic flavors. Opened June 2026 on the Lower East Side.

name: Eldridge Street Store
note: Late-night Chinese spot on the Lower East Side serving classics like peanut butter noodles and fried chicken wings. Open until 4am Thursday-Saturday. Opened June 2026.

name: Noury
note: Modern izakaya from the Kiko team (the Hudson Square restaurant that earned two NYT stars and an Esquire Best New Restaurants nod in 2025). 39-seat room at 137 Sullivan St. Multicultural team fusing local ingredients with premium Japanese ones. Opened June 2026.

name: Lala Hot Chicken
note: Nashville-Korean fried chicken from chef Jun Park (Chick Chick, Jun Men). Combos around $22 (tenders, sandwiches, sliders); hot chicken sando $16. Five heat levels from zero to Carolina reaper - lighter and more balanced than classic Korean fried chicken. Opened June 2026 in Chelsea.

name: Oberon
source: Time Out / Edible Manhattan / Infatuation July 2026
note: First Manhattan full-service restaurant from Henry Rich's Oberon Group (Rhodora, June, Rucola). Opened Tuesday July 7, 2026 inside the New Museum's OMA-designed ground floor at 235 Bowery, with a speakeasy-style night entrance through Freeman Alley. Exec chef Julia Sherman (Salad for President) and co-exec chef Ali Ghriskey — vegetable-forward, ingredient-driven cooking built on Hudson Valley producers; seasonal plates like fettuccine with peas and chanterelles. Open late for cocktails until 1am.

name: Badshah Social
source: The Infatuation / Park Slope Walk July 2026
note: Modern Indian restaurant-and-lounge hybrid from restaurateur Sharma, who previously ran the Badshah restaurants in Manhattan and runs Swagat on the UWS. Playful, hip Brooklyn debut on Flatbush Ave — crispy spinach chaat, Amritsari fish with housemade tartar sauce, lamb shank nihari; small plates and cocktails late into the night. Newly added to The Infatuation's July 2026 openings.

name: Pizza 4P's
note: US debut of the Vietnamese-founded, Japanese-owned Pizza 4P's chain (40+ locations across Vietnam, Cambodia, India, Indonesia and Japan). Opened July 2026 in the Greenpoint retail complex at 50 Norman Ave alongside Dashi Okume and Cafe O'Te. House-made cheeses and locally sourced ingredients, with creative pies like smoked duck and Meyer lemon burrata.

name: Taco Lane
note: Asian-Mexican fusion spot from chef Lane Li, opened Monday July 6, 2026 just off Union Square. Six Chinese-influenced taco varieties with house-crafted salsas and chili oil.

name: Maison Madison
address: 301 South End Ave, New York, NY 10280
note: Parisian salon-style bar and lounge from partner James Belakh, opened July 10, 2026 in Battery Park City. French-inspired American menu — French onion sliders and creative cocktails — plus a 'Friends of Maddie' membership program.

name: Hancock Cafe
note: Cafe connected to the artists' collective The Hancock on Rivington. La Cabra coffee and French-inspired light bites alongside records and books. Newly added to The Infatuation's July 2026 openings.

name: Frenchette Bakery Nolita
note: Third location of Frenchette Bakery from the Le Veau d'Or team, on Broome St in Nolita. Baguettes, jambon beurre, and laminated pastries. Newly added to The Infatuation's July 2026 openings.

name: Dokidoki
note: Latin-Japanese cocktail bar collaboration in Williamsburg. Low-ABV options and creative drinks like a cafecito with shochu. Newly added to The Infatuation's July 2026 openings.

name: Skinny Louie (East Village)
note: Fourth NYC location of the Miami-born smash-burger concept, slated to open July 24, 2026 in the East Village. Fresh Black Angus smash burgers; first 100 guests at 6pm on opening day get a complimentary burger.
